raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, contribution claim, and framing of ICRN above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The artifact contains an apparent contradiction about having no retrieval while later attributing gains to retrieved IPCC excerpts, so logical consistency is load-bearing.","R1.3":"The request plausibly requires aligning performance claims and causal claims with the reported evaluation and ablations.","R1.5":"Strengthening the passage includes improving the section-level narrative arc from problem, method, comparison, and results.","R1.6":"The broad phrasing also implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and register.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what specific kind of strengthening is intended by the terse delegation.","RX.2":"The artifact includes strong first-work and causal-performance claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.","RX.3":"The agent must only project responsibilities and not actually revise or critique the artifact.","RX.4":"Because the topic concerns municipal climate-risk narratives, avoiding unsupported guidance that could affect planning decisions is active.","RX.5":"Traceability is needed to ground the projection in the delegation text and the artifact’s stated claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, contribution claim, and framing of ICRN above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an apparent contradiction about having no retrieval while later attributing gains to retrieved IPCC excerpts, so logical consistency is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The request plausibly requires aligning performance claims and causal claims with the reported evaluation and ablations.
rationale.R1.5: Strengthening the passage includes improving the section-level narrative arc from problem, method, comparison, and results.
rationale.R1.6: The broad phrasing also implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what specific kind of strengthening is intended by the terse delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact includes strong first-work and causal-performance claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibilities and not actually revise or critique the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns municipal climate-risk narratives, avoiding unsupported guidance that could affect planning decisions is active.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is needed to ground the projection in the delegation text and the artifact’s stated claims.
